rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Stephen Homs Compliance With November 21, 2017 Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2018 line 1. PLAINTIFF PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC's Motion To Compel Defendant Stephen Hom's Compliance With November 21, 2017 Order GRANTED as follows. Defendant Stephen Hom, as trustee shall provide to the Court by no later than 9:00 a.m. on January 11, 2018 and to Plaintiff's counsel at the time of the hearing a redacted Trust document (verification may be submitted by January 16, 2018) showing only (1) the names of original trustee(s); (2) the names of successor trustee(s); and (3) the mechanism of successor trustee/appointment selection if the named successor trustee is not capable or not willing to serve (if any). Courtesy copy to be delivered to Department 501 no later than 9:00 a.m. on January 11, 2018. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANA MACDONALD VS. DUKE PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE, LLC'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to causes of action for breach of contract, B&P 17200, negligence (MacDonald), wrongful foreclosure (Brown). These causes of action are sufficiently alleged. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action for negligence (Brown) and wrongful foreclosure (MacDonald) as they are not proper plaintiffs under these causes of action. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action for violation of CC 2923.6 as Plaintiff alleges that no modification application was pending at the time of NOD, NOTS or sale. Plaintiff alleges that the application was approved and Plaintiff complied with the terms of the approval. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A FOR ITSELF AND AS SUCCESS VS. IRMA ENCINAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On The Cross-Complaint Of Irma Encinas
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2018 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On The Cross-Complaint Of Irma Encinas DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden under CCP 437c. Defendant relies on inadmissible evidence in support of its arguments and cites such evidence in the Separate Statement, e.g. FACT 1 citing to inadmissible Ex. 1; FACT 11 citing to Ex. 9 purportedly authenticated by virtue of the inadmissible Ex. 1. Failure to provide admissible competent evidence is not merely a procedural defect and is in violation of the CCP 437c mandatory requirements. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: YENEFER FERNANDO AGUILAR ET AL VS. MARK GE ZHANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Yenefer Fernando Aguilar And Dilia Castro'S Motion To Quash Production Of Business Records And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2018 line 9. Plaintiffs Yenefer Fernando Aguilar And Dilia Castro'S Motion To Quash Production Of Business Records And Request For Sanctions GRANTED in part and DENIED in part as follows: the subpoena is modified to provide for production of documents or portions thereof that relate to complaints or treatment for emotional distress from January 2010 to the present, including any documents related to the therapy group and social worker visit referred to in Castro's response to interrogatory 6.4. Quest shall provide to Plaintiffs' counsel, Jessica Alexander, copy of the documents Quest received as a result of the subpoena, which are to be numbered, and Quest shall retain the originals. Plaintiff's counsel shall inspect the documents to determine if any documents or portion thereof are outside the scope of the modified subpoena. Within fifteen days of receipt of the documents from Quest, Plaintiffs' counsel shall provide defense counsel, with those documents that come within the scope of the modified subpoena, including any necessary redactions for privileged material, together with a with a privileged log for any documents withheld or redacted. If defense counsel believes that additional documents should have been produced or that inappropriate redactions were made then defendants may bring a new motion to compel further responses. Plaintiffs' counsel shall also retain a copy of all records provided by Quest without redactions. The requests for sanctions are denied. = (501/REQ)
